methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
AAVantgarde Bio is a Series B-stage biotech company founded by Alberto Auricchio and Natalia Misciattelli that has raised $141M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 8 private companies and 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector, all at similar or proximate stages.

Two quantitative methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $141M total raised as a proxy baseline, implying a valuation of $268M. Peer-median benchmarking across 5 same-sector, same-stage private companies yields a median valuation of $810M, with a range of $600M (Profluent, Asimov) to $3.3B (Eikon Therapeutics). Public-comp anchoring using a 6.1x P/S median across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L cannot be applied due to absence of revenue data for the target. The triangulated analysis across the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $228M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $539M.

The wide spread between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects material uncertainty around which comparables best reflect AAVantgarde's risk and opportunity profile. The peer set shows significant dispersion; positioning within the $600M–$810M range may be most defensible absent proprietary diligence on clinical stage, IP strength, or market opportunity relative to named peers.
